name: service-installation
prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, magazine quality, NOT stock. Tight three-quarter angle of a craftsman holding a fresh continuous run of dark-bronze seamless aluminum gutter, bright copper-finish edge catching the soft Pacific Northwest overcast light. The gutter sweeps from upper-left foreground back into the frame toward a beautiful Pacific Northwest cedar-shake home in the background, slightly soft. Worker's gloved hands and weathered work jacket sleeve visible, no full face. Wet driveway, light mist, mature Doug fir trees behind the home. Color palette: deep money-green olive, weathered cedar warmth, dark bronze gutter, copper highlights. Photographed on Leica Q with 28mm, sharp gutter detail, dreamy soft background. No text, no logos, no faces. Documentary editorial like Magnum Photos.

name: service-replacement
prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, NOT stock. A two-story Pacific Northwest cedar-shake home with HALF its old failing gutters removed and HALF brand-new dark-bronze seamless gutters freshly installed — visible direct comparison along one continuous eave line. Old left side: weathered, sagging, water-stained gutter section being lowered down by ladder. Right side: crisp clean new bronze gutter perfectly hung. Two ladders against the home, one with the old gutter and one with the new. Misty PNW morning, soft overcast light, lush native landscaping. Color palette: deep money-green olive forest, warm cedar siding, dark bronze new gutter, weathered grey old gutter for contrast. Photographed on Hasselblad medium format, sharp detail on both gutter sections. No people in shot (workers off-frame), no text, no logos. Documentary editorial style.

name: service-guards
prompt: Macro editorial documentary photograph, magazine quality, NOT stock. Extreme close-up detail of a Pacific Northwest gutter system showing newly installed micro-mesh gutter guards with Doug fir needles, cedar debris, and fall maple leaves resting on top of the mesh — being shed perfectly while clean rainwater flows freely through underneath. Visible morning mist hanging in the air. The gutter is dark-bronze seamless aluminum. Behind the gutter line, the eave of a beautiful PNW home with cedar shake siding. Soft natural overcast PNW light catches the rain droplets and the bronze metal edge in copper highlights. Color palette: deep money-green olive moss tones, warm needle browns, dark bronze metal, copper accents. Photographed on Sony A7R V with 90mm macro, razor-sharp foreground gutter and debris detail, soft bokeh background. No text, no logos, no people. Documentary editorial.

name: service-soffit
prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, NOT stock, magazine quality. Mid-distance view of a Pacific Northwest craftsman home eave with the old gutter removed, exposing rotted weathered cedar fascia board on the LEFT half of the frame, and freshly replaced primed bright cedar fascia on the RIGHT half — clear before/after comparison along one continuous eave. A worker's gloved hands hold a level against the new fascia. Background: cedar-shake home wall, slightly soft. Soft overcast PNW light, hint of mist. Color palette: deep money-green olive trim and landscaping, warm honey new cedar, weathered grey old wood, hint of dark bronze where new gutter will be hung. Photographed on Leica Q 28mm, sharp fascia and tool detail. No full face visible, no text, no logos. Documentary editorial.

name: service-cleaning
prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, NOT stock. A worker on a tall extension ladder leaning against a Pacific Northwest cedar-shake home, hand-clearing a thick mat of Doug fir needles, cedar debris, and decaying maple leaves from a dark-bronze seamless gutter into a contractor bucket attached to the ladder. View from below at a low angle so the gutter line cuts dramatically across the frame. Worker's silhouetted upper body and gloved hands visible, no clear face. Misty grey PNW morning, soft overcast light, mature Doug fir branches in soft background. Color palette: deep money-green olive, warm cedar siding, dark bronze gutter, decaying needle and leaf browns. Photographed on Canon R5 with 35mm, sharp gutter and worker detail, soft sky bokeh. No text, no logos, no clear face. Documentary editorial style.

name: gallery-edmonds-craftsman
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a beautiful classic Edmonds, Washington Craftsman home with brand-new 6-inch dark-bronze K-style seamless gutters hung crisply along all rooflines and eaves. Cedar shake siding in warm honey tones, deep green trim, classic exposed-rafter craftsman porch. Mature laurel hedge in foreground, hint of Puget Sound water glimpsed in the distance through trees. Soft overcast Pacific Northwest light, slightly damp ground, calm. Color palette: deep money-green olive, warm cedar, dark bronze gutters, soft sound-mist blue distance. Photographed on medium format Hasselblad, sharp gutter line detail. No people, no cars, no text, no logos.

name: gallery-bellevue-modern
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a 1970s Bellevue, Washington split-level home recently refreshed with brand-new dark-bronze seamless aluminum gutters and copper-finish downspouts. Vertical cedar siding repainted in warm dark forest green, low-slope roofline, mid-century modern exposed beams. Manicured Eastside lawn in foreground, ornamental Japanese maple. Soft overcast PNW afternoon light, late-summer. Color palette: deep money-green olive, warm copper accents, modern dark trim. Photographed on Hasselblad medium format, sharp roofline and gutter detail. No people, no cars in driveway, no text, no logos.

name: gallery-mill-creek-guards
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a Mill Creek, Washington home shaded by a dramatic mature canopy of giant Doug fir and red cedar trees that nearly meet over the house. Brand-new dark-bronze seamless gutters with visible micro-mesh guards crisply installed along eaves and dormer rooflines. Cedar shake siding, dark trim, classic PNW family home. Misty morning light filters through the canopy in soft beams. Mossy ground, native sword fern landscaping in foreground. Color palette: deep money-green olive forest, mossy emerald, warm cedar, dark bronze gutters. Photographed on medium format. No people, no cars, no text, no logos.

name: gallery-snohomish-farmhouse
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a classic Snohomish County, Washington farmhouse with newly rebuilt cedar fascia and brand-new dark-bronze seamless aluminum gutters along all eaves. White-painted board-and-batten siding, dark green trim, wide wraparound porch, weathered standing-seam metal roof. Open pasture or hay field in foreground with split-rail wood fence. Mature maple trees beside the house. Soft late-afternoon golden hour PNW light, calm. Color palette: deep money-green olive trim and field, weathered white siding, warm honey cedar fascia, dark bronze gutters. Photographed on Hasselblad medium format. No people, no animals, no text, no logos.

name: gallery-kirkland-waterfront
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a sophisticated Kirkland, Washington lakefront contemporary home with brand-new 6-inch dark-bronze seamless gutters and prominent COPPER-FINISH downspouts at the corners — copper catches warm late-afternoon light. Mixed cedar and stone siding, large picture windows facing Lake Washington (visible in soft background, calm water reflecting sky). Manicured Eastside landscaping with ornamental grasses. Color palette: deep money-green olive landscaping, warm cedar, copper highlights, soft lake blue distance. Photographed on Hasselblad medium format, sharp copper downspout detail. No people, no boats, no text, no logos.

name: gallery-lynnwood-twostory
prompt: Editorial architectural photograph, NOT stock. Three-quarter view of a two-story Lynnwood, Washington traditional family home receiving a complete gutter replacement — one continuous run of brand-new dark-bronze seamless aluminum gutters now traces every eave perfectly. Cedar shake siding warm honey tones, dark trim. Misty Pacific Northwest morning, low fog still hanging in the front lawn and trees. Wet driveway in foreground reflects the home. Mature Doug fir trees behind. Color palette: deep money-green olive landscaping and trees, warm cedar, dark bronze gutters, soft grey mist. Photographed on Hasselblad medium format, sharp gutter and roofline detail. No people, no cars, no text, no logos.

name: whypnw-rain-on-gutter
prompt: Cinematic editorial photograph, NOT stock, magazine cover quality. Wide three-quarter view of a luxury modern Pacific Northwest residence during a steady refreshing rain shower at golden hour. Sophisticated mixed materials: dark vertical-stained cedar siding, warm cedar shake gable, large floor-to-ceiling windows glowing warm amber inside. Crisp focus on the long roofline where rainwater is flowing perfectly into clean dark-bronze seamless gutters with NO overflowing — system working perfectly. Visible water cascading off the gutter into a copper-finish downspout in a clean controlled sheet. Soft individual rain droplets visible against the warm directional light, frozen mid-fall like slow-motion. Lush wet PNW landscaping in foreground (sword fern, cedar, moss). Background of mature Doug fir rising into low rolling fog. Wet stone walkway reflects the warm interior light in shimmering streaks. Atmosphere calm and reassuring — the picture of 'your home is protected.' Color palette: deep money-green olive forest, warm amber-copper interior light catching bronze gutters, soft sage mist, dark wet wood. Cinematic film grain, photographed on Arri Alexa with Cooke 35mm prime, sharp gutter detail, soft background bokeh. No people, no vehicles, no text, no logos.
